titlestmt.titleproper: National Historic Preservation Program oral histories4327
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-01-21 18:27:52 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: National Historic Preservation Program oral histories
did.abstract: Interviews (59) by James A. Glass on the subject of the development of the National Historic Preservation Program between 1957 and 1980. Interviewees include Lawrence E. Aten, Edwin C. Bearss, Martha M. Bigelow, Tersh Boasberg, J.O. Brew, Helen D. Bullock, John A. Burns, James H. Charleton, S. Allen Chambers, Jr., Ernest Allen Connally, Chris T. Delaporte, Eric N. DeLony, Antoinette F. Downing, Carl Feiss, Robert G. Ferris, John Fowler, Russell W. Fridley, Robert R. Garvey, Jr., George B. Hartzog, Jr., Gary L. Hume, Casey and Lucille Ireland, H. Ward Jandl, George A. Karas, Russell V. Keune, Thomas F. King, Bennie C. Keel, Lawson B. Knott, Jr., James M. Lambe, Ben Levy, Nellie L. Longworth, Elizabeth A. Lyon, James C. Massey, John D. McDermott, Lee McElvain, Terry B. Morton, William Penn Mott, Jr., William J. Murtagh, Lee H. Nelson, Loretta Neumann, Stephen D. Newman, Sally G. Oldham, Mary Carolyn Pitts, Constance Werner Ramirez, Dwight F. Rettie, Jerry L. Rogers, Douglas H. Scovill, Horace J. Sheely, Jr., Carol D. Shull, Ann Webster Smith, Larry E. Tise, Merle Wells, Thomas B. Williams, Frederick C. Williamson, and Alfred B. Wolfe.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Individual interviews may have specific restrictions.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to Ben Levy interview restricted until permission is granted to open.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to William J. Murtagh interview restricted during lifetime of interviewee or by his written permission.

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Oral history tapes were compiled as research for a Ph.D. dissertation.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: National Historic Preservation Program oral histories, #4327. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Interviews (59) by James A. Glass on the subject of the development of the National Historic Preservation Program between 1957 and 1980. Interviewees include Lawrence E. Aten, Edwin C. Bearss, Martha M. Bigelow, Tersh Boasberg, J.O. Brew, Helen D. Bullock, John A. Burns, James H. Charleton, S. Allen Chambers, Jr., Ernest Allen Connally, Chris T. Delaporte, Eric N. DeLony, Antoinette F. Downing, Carl Feiss, Robert G. Ferris, John Fowler, Russell W. Fridley, Robert R. Garvey, Jr., George B. Hartzog, Jr., Gary L. Hume, Casey and Lucille Ireland, H. Ward Jandl, George A. Karas, Russell V. Keune, Thomas F. King, Bennie C. Keel, Lawson B. Knott, Jr., James M. Lambe, Ben Levy, Nellie L. Longworth, Elizabeth A. Lyon, James C. Massey, John D. McDermott, Lee McElvain, Terry B. Morton, William Penn Mott, Jr., William J. Murtagh, Lee H. Nelson, Loretta Neumann, Stephen D. Newman, Sally G. Oldham, Mary Carolyn Pitts, Constance Werner Ramirez, Dwight F. Rettie, Jerry L. Rogers, Douglas H. Scovill, Horace J. Sheely, Jr., Carol D. Shull, Ann Webster Smith, Larry E. Tise, Merle Wells, Thomas B. Williams, Frederick C. Williamson, and Alfred B. Wolfe.

Subjects include the National Historic Preservation Act of 1966, the National Park Service, the National Trust for Historic Preservation, the Advisory Council on Historic Preservation, the National Conference of State Historic Preservation Officers, the Office of Archeology and Historic Preservation of the National Park Service, the Heritage Conservation and Recreation Service, Preservation Action, Inc., the U.S. Conference of Mayors' Special Committee on Historic Preservation (Rains Committee), the Tax Reform Act of 1976, Executive Order 11593 of 1971, and the 1980 amendments to the National Historic Preservation Act.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on use:
userestrict.p: Tersh Boasberg interview: citations and quotations require written permission until 2004 or during lifetime of interviewee.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Helen D. Bullock interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.|Russell V. Keune interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Nellie L. Longworth interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.

userestrict.p: Lee McElvain interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.

userestrict.p: Terry B. Morton interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: William Penn Mott, Jr. interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Sally G. Oldham interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Constance Werner Ramirez interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Carol D. Shull interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Ann Webster Smith interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Larry E. Tise interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Thomas B. Williams interview: citations and quotation require written permission during lifetime of interview.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Albert B. Wolfe interview: citations and quotations require written permission of interviewee.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Elizabeth A. Lyon interview: citations and quotations require written permission during lifetime of interviewee.

controlaccess.subject: Architecture -- United States -- Conservation and restoration.
Historic sites -- Law and legislation -- United States.
Historic buildings -- United States -- Conservation and restoration.

United States. Advisory Council on Historic Preservation
United States. Office of Archeology and Historic Preservation
United States. Heritage Conservation and Recreation Service
United States Conference of Mayors. Special Committee on Historic Preservation
United States. National Historic Preservation Act of 1966
United States. National Historic Preservation Act Amendments of 1980
United States. Tax Reform Act of 1976

did.unittitle: Series I. Transcripts and Computer Disks
